
Scanned Grok social signals and arXiv RSS signals. The arXiv papers are mostly generic AI research with no Claude-specific practical tips. From the Grok signals, two items stand out as genuinely novel and not already in the README: the XML prompting technique (Simon Willison, credible source, non-obvious) and the Claude Projects productivity workflow (Anthropic-adjacent, broad applicability). The computer use signal overlaps with existing README entries. The Artifacts post lacks a stable primary URL. Proposing 2 high-confidence additions.

## Suggestions

- [Advanced XML Prompting for Claude](https://simonwillison.net/2024/Oct/5/xml-prompting-claude/) — Simon Willison's documented technique for using XML tags to structure Claude prompts, with reported 20–30% accuracy improvements on complex tasks — grounded in hands-on experimentation with concrete before/after examples.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche), Novel or non-obvious — not a basic feature already in official docs

- [Productivity Hacks with Claude Projects](https://www.anthropic.com/news/projects) — Anthropic's documented workflow for using Claude Projects to maintain persistent context, organize long-running tasks across sessions, and share instructions across chats — a non-obvious capability with strong week-over-week productivity payoff.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche)
